authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85 — Trout (General Statewide Regulations)
sources.quotedText: (a) General Statewide Regulations: (1) All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: (87) Martis Creek from the Martis Lake dam downstream to the confluence with the Truckee River (Nevada Co.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout. (88) Martis Lake tributaries (Nevada and Placer cos.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout. [No separate 'Martis Lake' (the reservoir itself) entry appears in the current §7.50(b) list.]

sources.title: CDFW Sierra District trout regulation simplification — rulemaking analysis (Martis Lake)
sources.quotedText: (112) Martis Lake and tributaries (Nevada and Placer Cos.) (A) Martis Lake. Last Saturday in Apr. through Nov. 15. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 [trout] -> Move to statewide reg / Move to statewide reg for lakes and reservoirs SL. (B) Martis Lake tributaries. Closed to all fishing all year -> All year.

sources.title: Martis Creek Lake | U.S. Army Corps of Engineers
sources.quotedText: Martis Creek Lake was the first "catch and release trophy trout" lake established in California. Varieties include Rainbow, Brown and Lahonton Cutthroat trout. Anglers must use flies, barbless hooks and artificial lures only. Live bait is not permitted. No fishing is allowed in the streams above the lake.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Verified against the live CCR §7.50(b) text (current through Register 2026, No. 25): there is NO standalone 'Martis Lake' entry — only (87) Martis Creek below the dam and (88) Martis Lake tributaries, both 0-trout. The reservoir itself follows the statewide lake default. Secondary/tourism sources (incl. USACE's recreation page) describing the lake as a 0-limit trophy water are outdated.

regulations.humanSummary: Open to fishing all year (general statewide lake default — the reservoir itself has no §7.50(b) special listing; verified against the current CCR text)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout/day, 10 in possession under the general statewide lake default. The lake's former special 0-trout catch-and-release listing was moved to the statewide lake rule; verified against the live CCR §7.50(b) text (no 'Martis Lake' entry; only the creek below the dam and the tributaries remain specially listed at 0 trout). Widely repeated secondary-source claims that the lake remains a 0-limit trophy water (USACE's page, angling sites) are outdated.
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
